_description: Reference snapshot for a Tauri project (statefulRuntime='tauri') with node_modules + target/ build artifacts + vite.config.ts file watcher. Indicators contributing: (1) statefulRuntime=tauri, (2) file watcher present, (3) two shared build-artifact dirs. Reaches antiFitScore >= 3 even without a bound port. Tauri's WebView ↔ Rust bridge holds runtime state (windows, FS handles, IPC channels) that does NOT survive being run from a sibling worktree; spawning worktree workers against a live tauri dev process risks WebView/process desync. Canonical 'stateful runtime' anti-fit case.

_lineage.context: The 2026-04-26 SessionHelm/WinLocksmith Tauri-port-collision incident motivated the strictPort:true rule in MACHINE_CONFIG.md Dev Port Registry; the same incident demonstrates why Tauri projects are categorically anti-fit for worktree mode.

antiFitReasons: multiple shared build-artifact dirs present: node_modules, target

expectedHeuristicVerdict: anti-fit-warn (Tauri WebView/Rust bridge state is incompatible with worktree isolation — recommend same-branch even when file-touches are disjoint)
_match: Field-shape match. statefulRuntime exact match required ('tauri'). antiFitScore floor (>=3) required.
